key_insight: D-gun sprayed Al2O3–TiO2 coatings significantly improve the wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V, and random forest most accurately predicts wear from sliding conditions.
engineering_relevance: The study shows a practical route to extend the service life of aerospace, biomedical, and marine Ti-6Al-4V components by combining thermal-spray coating with data-driven wear prediction.
method: Applied ~200 µm Al2O3–TiO2 coating on Ti-6Al-4V using detonation gun spraying.
Ran ASTM G99 pin-on-disc wear tests at 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min against EN31 steel.
Trained ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from test parameters.
evidence_signal: Wear tests and SEM observations showed reduced wear and protective tribo-layer formation on coated samples, while random forest achieved the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error among the models.
watch_item: The predictive models appear to be trained on a limited experimental range, so performance may drop when extrapolating beyond the tested loads, speeds, or coating conditions.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
